title: Deep non-tail recursion (~270k frames) dies with SIGILL and no diagnostic
goal: Found by the P4 stress harness (2026-07-02, master 2692ca63). Plain user non-tail recursion past ~270k frames (JIT; ~280k AOT) dies with SIGILL and NO error message. The runtime guard ESHKOL_MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH=100000 (lib/core/runtime_list_helpers.cpp eshkol_check_recursion_depth) only covers runtime list helpers, so user recursion never hits it - the failure mode is a silent trap instead of 'maximum recursion depth exceeded'. depth 260000 passes both paths (pinned by tests/stress/rec_deep_nontco_250k.esk at 250k). Fix direction: stack-probe + diagnostic on overflow, or extend the depth guard to user-function prologues under a flag.
acceptance: depth-300k recursion either completes or reports a recursion/stack diagnostic with nonzero exit (tests/stress/found/deep_recursion_270k_no_diagnostic.esk XPASSes)
campaign_payoff: Turns the single worst-to-debug failure mode (silent SIGILL) into a diagnosable error.
